description: Stable claim key for audit and gate issues.
Originating report unit identifier.
Reader-facing section title where the claim appears or is audited.
Stable section key used for planner and draft alignment.
Identifier used to connect final prose back to this claim.
Optional first rendered line for the claim span.
Optional last rendered line for the claim span.
Optional start offset for the exact claim span within the final prose unit.
Optional end offset for the exact claim span within the final prose unit.
Exact text captured from the claim-bearing prose unit.
Normalized form of `exact_text_span` used for dedupe and contract checks.
Claim-kind used for source-role requirements and release gating.
Risk tier for the claim itself.
Evaluated support state after evidence mapping and guard stages.
Whether the claim remains in mainline reader-facing prose.
Distinguishes report-and-audit from audit-only claims.
Source identifiers cited for this claim.
Observed source roles attached to the cited sources.
Required source roles for this claim kind and risk profile.
Subset of required roles that the cited evidence actually satisfies.
Original finding identifiers that fed the claim.
Human-readable labels for grounded source spans feeding the claim.
Start offsets for grounded source spans feeding the claim.
End offsets for grounded source spans feeding the claim.
Inspectable marker showing whether the claim is grounded to a direct document span or multispan block.
Scope-limiting note carried from grounded findings into the claim ledger.
Trace state for prose-to-claim lineage such as `linked`, `mismatch`, or `audit_only`.
Count of cited evidence items supporting the claim record.
Concrete action needed to fix blocked or weak support.
Inspectable reasons that keep a claim from release.
Inspectable reasons a claim stays out of mainline prose.
Typed absence basis when the claim is an absence claim.
Optional jurisdiction carried with the claim.
